# Launcher/view QA-tester checklists (#10719 / #10722)

QA-tester-level checklists for every launcher view, settings subview, chat, dashboard, and widget. Each file enumerates checkable items across 10 dimensions: nav/entry, primary interactions, state matrix (empty/loading/error/offline/permission/guest/overflow), repeated/rapid-fire idempotency, back-and-forth switching + state recovery, fuzz/adversarial input, input modalities (mouse/keyboard/touch/context-menu/DnD), a11y/geometry (focus/44px/axe/hover-color), concurrency/races, and per-item COVERED/PARTIAL/GAP status against committed specs.

Authored by a 12-agent fan-out that grepped real component props + existing coverage. These drive Phase 3 implementation (each GAP item → a test).

## Checklists & biggest gap per group

- **[Launcher & Home Shell](launcher-home-shell.md)** — biggest coverage gap: `KioskViewCanvas` and the entire shell-mode router (`readShellMode` → Kiosk/TrayPopover/ChatOverlay/OnboardingOverlay shells) have **zero committed tests** — active-surface selection, the iframe sandbox lock that stops a view replacing the kiosk shell, floating-window drag, and which shell each `?shellMode=` value boots are all unverified. A close second is `NotificationCente
- **[Chat Surfaces](chat.md)** — biggest coverage gap: the overlay is superbly covered at the jsdom unit/fuzz level (100+ overlay tests, 31 fuzz, 16 slash, full detent state-machine and gesture invariants) but has essentially **no real-browser a11y or visual-invariant verification** — no axe pass after interaction, no 44px tap-target audit, no hover-color (orange→darker, no-blue) check, and reduced-motion/pinch/grabber-keyboard a
- **[Dashboard & Widgets](dashboard-widgets.md)** — biggest coverage gap: the group is unit-strong but interaction-weak — only one e2e (`home-widget-priority.spec.ts`) touches the live home and it asserts ranking + screenshots only, so no committed test ever taps a card, dismisses the FTU widget, checks keyboard/focus/axe/44px targets, proves rapid-fire nav idempotency, or exercises the `WidgetErrorBoundary` fallback; the Todo widget has stories bu
- **[Settings — Agent group](settings-agent.md)** — biggest coverage gap: **VoiceConfigView is effectively orphaned/untested** — the task's named `voice` component (TTS provider picker, wake-word config, ASR override, save) has no route and no behavioral test, because the live `/settings#voice` section actually mounts `VoiceSectionMount`→`VoiceSection` instead; the only voice specs (`VoiceSection.test.tsx`, `voice-desktop-selftest.spec.ts`) exercis
- **[Settings — System & Security groups](settings-system-security.md)** — biggest coverage gap is **RemotePluginHostSection**, which has zero behavior coverage — install/start/stop/logs/uninstall-with-confirm and its two live desktop-bridge subscriptions are entirely untested despite being a high-risk surface (a remote plugin "can call the app's API as you"); SecuritySettingsSection (remote-password setup/change and session revocation, auth-critical, render-only) is a c
- **[Data & Runtime Views](data-views.md)** — biggest coverage gap: three data views — **LogsView, RelationshipsWorkspaceView, and RuntimeView** — have zero dedicated unit or e2e specs; their only "coverage" is the generic screenshot-walk that asserts render-without-crash, not any filter/merge/expand semantics. RelationshipsWorkspaceView is the most dangerous since its candidate-merge approve/reject mutates the identity graph through the merg
- **[Media & Device Views](media-device-views.md)** — biggest gap: the tab-routed `/phone`, `/messages`, `/contacts` ElizaOsAppsView surfaces have **zero behavioral component/e2e coverage** — only the `ensureNativeReadGranted` unit decision-table exists. The one deterministic comms e2e (`apps-comms-device-interactions.spec.ts`) tests a *different* native `/apps/phone|messages|contacts` `*-shell` surface, so every real control (dialpad, place-call, SM
- **[Character & Automation Authoring](character-authoring.md)** — biggest coverage gap is the **Custom Actions Editor** — the app's most security-sensitive surface (authors and executes arbitrary HTTP/shell/code plus AI-generate and a save-then-Test flow) yet has only a Storybook render smoke test, with zero behavioral coverage of validation, handler-type switching, the Test/execute path, toggle idempotency, or adversarial inputs (SSRF/`javascript:` URLs, `rm -r
- **[Apps / Views / Overlay Surfaces](apps-views-surfaces.md)** — biggest coverage gap is `GameViewOverlay`'s cross-origin `postMessage` auth handshake: it's a privileged iframe that posts an auth payload, yet has zero behavioral tests (only a wiring reference in `App.navigate-view-wiring.test.tsx`), so origin/source spoof rejection, the fail-closed no-target-origin path, and the one-shot `authSentRef` guard are all unverified — and its drag is `onMouseDown`-onl
- **[Onboarding, Auth & Help](onboarding-auth.md)** — biggest coverage gap is the connection-recovery tier: `ConnectionFailedBanner` and `ConnectionLostOverlay` have zero committed tests and `CloudHandoffBanner` has only a hook-level test, so their single-flight/dismiss-latch idempotency guards — the race-prone logic that matters most during backend loss — are entirely unverified. Secondary standout gap: `LoginView`'s `PasswordTab` (submit/error/reme
- **[Background & Appearance](background-appearance.md)** — biggest coverage gap: `useBackgroundApplyChannel` — the sole chat→background bridge and the one place that normalizes untrusted agent payloads (`op: set/undo/reset`, malformed color/url) — has zero unit coverage; it's only touched indirectly by the screenshot e2e, so the `reset` op, image-via-chat, and adversarial/partial-payload paths are unverified. Secondary systemic gaps: no route/nav-entry te
- **[Cross-Cutting Interaction Fuzz & State Recovery](cross-cutting-fuzz.md)** — biggest gap: orientation change is entirely uncovered by any functional test (only the aesthetic screenshot audit touches it), and connection-loss recovery has no true end-to-end coverage — the two specs that would prove it (`multi-client-desync.spec.ts` and `multi-window-sync.spec.ts`) are both `test.skip` because the ui-smoke mock layer has no shared backing store, and no e2e ever forces `backen
